Abeyton Lodge · Internal Reference · Annotated Edition

Staff & Admin
Annotated Guide

Every page shown as a full screenshot. Find the red letter on the image, then read what it does below.

Sign InAdminFloor View OrdersCalendarOverlord
Sign In● Create Account● Admin Page● Floor View● Seating Mode● Table Colors● Table Panel● Orders● Edit Tab● Calendar● Overlord
01
Login Screen
What you see when you open the app at abbeyton-lodge.netlify.app

The left side is the Abeyton Lodge branding panel. Your login form is on the right. Staff and Admin use the same login page.

001 — Login Screen
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Demo account shortcuts
On the demo/dev site only — quick-login buttons grouped by role (Staff, Member, Admin). Click any name to auto-fill that account's credentials. In production, you won't see these buttons.
B
Email & password form
Type your staff or admin email in the EMAIL field. Enter your password below. Click SHOW to reveal it as you type. Click SIGN IN to log in.
C
"Request access" link
Don't have an account? Click here to go to the Create Account page. The Staff Invite Code is STAFF2026. You'll get this from your manager before signing up.
02
Create Account
How to register a new staff account.

This form creates your login. If you leave the Staff Invite Code blank, you'll be registered as a regular Member — you won't have access to Admin, Floor View, or Overlord.

002 — Create Account
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Email and password fields
Fill in your email address. Choose a strong password and repeat it in the Confirm Password field below.
B
Staff Invite Code
Staff must fill this in. Leave blank only if you're a member. The invite code is what sets your account role to Staff rather than Member. Get the code from management. If you missed it, an Admin can fix your role later via Overlord → Users. Invite code is STAFF2026.
C
CREATE ACCOUNT button
Submits the form and creates your account. You'll be redirected to sign in. Already registered? Click Sign in at the very bottom of the page.
03
Admin Page
Your daily operations hub. All of today's reservations in one screen.

Get here via ADMIN in the top nav. This is where you confirm reservations, assign tables, check order status, read member messages, and cancel reservations.

003 — Admin Page
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Top navigation bar
Links across the top: FLOOR · ADMIN · OVERLORD · CALENDAR. The current page shows in bold. Staff accounts see FLOOR · ADMIN · CALENDAR only — no Overlord tab.
B
Your account info and role badge
Your email address and role chip display here. ADMIN = full access including Overlord. STAFF = no Overlord. Click → SIGN OUT to log out at the end of your shift.
C
Date navigator
The ← and → arrows move one day at a time. The TODAY button jumps back to today's date. You can also click directly on the date field and type in any date.
D
RESERVATIONS and FIRED ORDERS tabs
RESERVATIONS (N) — today's main list. FIRED ORDERS (N) — orders that have been sent to the kitchen and are waiting to be delivered and marked fulfilled. The number in parentheses is the live count.
E
Status filter row: ALL / CONFIRMED / DRAFT / CANCELLED
Filters the reservation list below. The number badge on each tab shows how many reservations are in that status today. Always check DRAFT — those need to be confirmed before the party arrives.
F
The reservation card
Each card shows: time, reservation #, guest count, party names. Status chips appear below the names:
📍 Room — Table   🔥 Orders fired   ✓ Orders fulfilled   ⚠ No order placed   💬 N unread messages
The progress bar beneath shows which lifecycle stage this reservation is at.
G
Status chip (top-right corner of the card)
Shows the reservation's current status: CONFIRMED, DRAFT, or CANCELLED. A Draft must be changed to Confirmed before seating the party.
H
REASSIGN and REMOVE buttons
These appear after a table has been assigned. REASSIGN opens the table picker to choose a different table. REMOVE unassigns the table entirely and puts the reservation back to unassigned.
I
CANCEL and DETAILS buttons
CANCEL changes the reservation status to Cancelled — it does NOT delete the record, just marks it cancelled. DETAILS opens the full edit modal where you can change date, time, status, attendees, notes, and read/reply to messages.
⚠️
Draft ≠ Confirmed. Members book as Draft by default. You must open DETAILS, set Status to Confirmed, and click SAVE RESERVATION before the party arrives — otherwise they won't appear correctly on the floor.
04
Floor View — Overview
The visual map of all dining rooms and tables. Navigate here via FLOOR in the top nav.

This is your main working screen during service. Seat parties, click tables to manage orders, move reservations between tables — all from here.

004 — Floor View Overview
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Title, color legend, and date navigator
"Floor View" heading top-left. Date navigation (← date → TODAY) top-right. Below the title: the color legend bar — Empty · Seated · Ordering · Partial · All Fired. These colors match the table card borders throughout the page.
B
UNSEATED section
All confirmed reservations that haven't been given a table yet appear here at the top. Each card shows: reservation # · time · party size, the member's notes (e.g. "Hangin' with Nolan"), the primary member's name, and their preferred dining room. Scroll down after seating to see the floor map.
D
SEAT button
Appears on the right side of every unseated reservation card. Click it to enter Seating Mode for that party — then click any available (gray) table on the floor map below to seat them.
E
Dining room sections
Each room appears as a labeled section: Breakfast Nook, Card Room, Croquet Court, Living Room, Pool. Tables within each room are shown as colored cards. Scroll down to see all rooms.
05
Floor View — Seating Mode
What the screen looks like after you click SEAT. You must complete or cancel before doing anything else.

You're now "holding" a reservation and looking for a table for it. The page won't let you do anything else until you either seat the party or cancel.

005 — Seating Mode Active
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
PICKED — the selected reservation
The reservation you clicked SEAT on now has an orange border and shows the PICKED badge. This is the party you're seating. All their info is visible: notes, member name, preferred room.
B
Seating mode banner
A banner appears at the very top of the page. It tells you which reservation is being seated (# · time · party size) and gives the instruction: "Click an Available table to seat." The banner stays visible as you scroll.
C
CANCEL button
Click this in the top-right of the banner to exit seating mode without making any changes. The reservation stays in the UNSEATED list.
D
Table rooms below (scroll down to see)
The dining room sections appear below the unseated list. Available tables will show a green glowing border — click any one to seat the party there.
06
Table Color States
Each table card's border color tells you what's happening at that table right now.

Glance at the floor map and instantly know which tables need attention — ordering, fired, or empty.

006 — Dining Rooms with Occupied and Empty Tables
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Breakfast Nook — TABLE 1 (green border = Seated / All Fired)
Green means either: the party is seated with no orders yet, OR all orders have been fired. This table shows 3/6 seats occupied, $0.00 estimated — no orders placed yet. Three dots below = three guests' order status indicators.
B
Card Room — TABLE 1 (orange border = Ordering)
Orange means orders exist but not all have been fired. 3/10 seats, $38.00 estimated. The colored dots show each individual guest's order state. Click this table to open the right panel and manage their orders.
C
Empty rooms — Croquet Court, Living Room, Pool tables
Tables showing "AVAILABLE" in gray have no reservation assigned. They're open for seating a new party — either through the UNSEATED list above or via REASSIGN on the Admin page.
ℹ️
Full color reference: Gray = Empty · Blue = Seated, no orders · Yellow/Gold = Ordering · Orange = Some fired, some open · Green = All fired
07
Floor View — Table Panel
Click any occupied table to open this side panel. Manage orders, edit the reservation, move the party.

Everything you need for a table is in this panel — you don't need to leave the floor to manage orders or edit a reservation.

007 — Right Panel Opened
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
The occupied table (orange = Ordering)
The table you clicked. Its color reflects its current status. Shows: table name, seat count, occupancy (e.g. 3/10), and estimated order total ($38.00).
B
MOVE TABLE button
Click to enter Move Mode. The page will prompt you to click any available table — the entire reservation (party, orders, attendees) moves there instantly.
C
Reservation summary card
The reservation currently at this table: date · time, guest count, estimated total, and status chip. Confirms you've clicked the right table.
D
ORDERS and EDIT tabs
ORDERS tab (default, shown selected) — add items, fire orders, print chits. EDIT tab — change reservation date, time, status, notes, and attendees without leaving the floor.
E
SEATS row — the guest circles
One numbered circle per guest (e.g. 1 DORRIE, 2 JOSH, 3 ROSIE). Click any circle to view and manage that guest's order. Circle color = their order status: empty outline = no order, gold fill = open order, green fill = fired/fulfilled.
F
Available tables (gray, below)
Tables with no border and "AVAILABLE" label are open. They're highlighted when you enter Move Mode or Seating Mode.
08
Orders Tab — Adding Items & Firing
After clicking a guest's seat circle, this is what you see. Add menu items and fire when ready.

Each guest has their own individual order. Click their circle, add their items, then fire. Repeat for each guest at the table.

008 — Orders Panel with Guest Order and Menu
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
ORDERS tab + SEATS row
The ORDERS tab is active (underlined). The SEATS row shows circles for each guest — 1 DORRIE, 2 JOSH, 3 ROSIE. The gold-filled circle is the currently selected guest. Click any circle to switch to that guest's order.
B
Guest order section
Shows the selected guest's name (Dorrie Goodman), their order status badge (OPEN = not yet fired), and their ORDER items with quantity × price and a running total. Click the × on any item to remove it.
C
ADD section — the full menu
Scroll down past the current order to see the full menu. Click any item's name or row to add one to this guest's order. Items are listed with their prices.
D
🔥 FIRE ORDER button
The big orange button. Click when this guest's order is complete and ready for the kitchen. This locks the order — no changes after firing. Status changes to FIRED and the kitchen can now see it.
⚠️
OPEN = the kitchen cannot see this order yet. You must click FIRE ORDER to send it. Don't tell a guest their food is on the way until their order is FIRED.
09
EDIT Tab & Messages
Switch from ORDERS to EDIT to modify reservation details or read member messages — without leaving the floor.

The EDIT tab is essential during service when a member asks to change their time, you need to update their status, or you want to read a message they sent.

009 — EDIT Tab with Reservation Fields and Messages
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
ORDERS / EDIT tabs
The EDIT tab is now active (orange underline). Click ORDERS to go back to the order management view. Both tabs are always accessible from the table panel.
B
Messages badge
A 💬 2 badge appears when the member has sent messages. The number is the unread count. Click the badge to open the message thread and read what they wrote — special requests, allergy alerts, arrival changes, etc.
C
Reservation edit fields
Editable fields: DATE, TIME, STATUS (Confirmed / Draft / Cancelled dropdown), DINING ROOM PREFERENCE dropdown, NOTES text area. All are editable directly here.
D
SAVE RESERVATION button
The orange button at the bottom. You must click this after making any changes — otherwise your edits are lost when you close the panel.
E
ATTENDEES section
Lists every guest for this reservation with their dietary restrictions shown below their name (e.g. shellfish_allergy). Click EDIT to update a guest's name or dietary info. Click DEL to remove that guest from this reservation.
10
Calendar
Month-view overview. Good for spotting busy days and jumping to future reservations.

Navigate here via CALENDAR in the top nav. Click any day that has dots on it to see that day's reservations in the right panel.

010 — Calendar Month View with Day Panel
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does — colors in the calendar dots match the legend below
A
Month navigation and TODAY button
The ← and → arrows move one month at a time. TODAY returns to the current month. The current day is highlighted.
B
Day dots — colored by reservation status
● Green = Confirmed reservation   ● Gold = Draft reservation   ● Red = Cancelled. Multiple dots on one day = multiple reservations. A busy day has many dots.
C
Day panel (right side)
Click any day to open this panel. Shows all reservations for that day: time, party size, room preference, primary member name, and notes. Scroll if there are many reservations.
D
DETAILS link on each reservation
Click DETAILS → on any reservation in the day panel to open the full edit modal — same as clicking DETAILS on the Admin page.
11
Overlord Panel
Admin-only. Direct access to every record in the database — all dates, all users.
⚠️
Overlord is a raw database interface. Admins only. There are no "Are you sure?" dialogs. Deleting a reservation here removes it and everything attached to it — attendees, orders, messages — instantly and permanently.

Use Overlord when you need to do something the Admin page or Floor View can't do — like changing a user's role, deactivating a menu item, or deleting a test reservation.

011 — Overlord Reservation Detail Modal
Find the red letter on the screenshot above, then read what it does
A
Edit fields in the detail modal
Date, time, status dropdown (Confirmed/Draft/Cancelled), and notes. Accessible by clicking DETAIL on any row in the Overlord reservations table. Click SAVE CHANGES to apply.
B
Messages section
Full message thread between this member and staff. Shows sender email and timestamp for every message. Type in the box and click SEND to reply. Click × next to any message to delete it.
C
ATTENDEES section
Every guest on this reservation with their dietary restrictions. EDIT to change name or dietary. DEL removes that guest from this reservation permanently.
D
SAVE RESERVATION button
Saves all field changes made in this modal. Always click this before closing the modal.
E
Add new attendee form
Type a name and optionally set dietary restrictions to add a guest to this reservation directly from Overlord.

Other Overlord Sections

Orders — view all orders, fire, fulfill, or delete individual orders
Menu — add new items, edit prices/descriptions, deactivate seasonal items
💡
To deactivate a seasonal menu item without deleting it: Overlord → Menu → click EDIT on the item → set Active to false → save. The item disappears from the member ordering screen but stays in the database for historical order records.
ABEYTON LODGE · ANNOTATED ADMIN GUIDE · INTERNAL USE ONLY
